summary: The test run crashed with a native segmentation fault during numpy.argsort called from a datetime test (test_datetime_nat_argsort_stability).
root_cause: A segmentation fault occurred inside numpy.argsort (via numpy._core.fromnumeric._wrapfunc -> argsort) while running numpy/_core/tests/test_datetime.py::test_datetime_nat_argsort_stability, causing the pytest job to abort.

evidence.reason: Shows the run terminated due to a native segmentation fault.
Stack frame indicates the crash happened while executing numpy._core.fromnumeric._wrapfunc.
Stack frame shows numpy.argsort was on the call stack at the time of the crash.
Identifies the specific test that triggered the call to argsort which crashed.
CI runner exit code confirms abnormal termination due to the segmentation fault.

suggested_fix: Reproduce the failure locally by running the single test: pytest -q numpy/_core/tests/test_datetime.py::test_datetime_nat_argsort_stability. Run under a native debugger or sanitizer (e.g. build with debug symbols and ASAN or run under gdb/valgrind) to get a C-level backtrace. Investigate the implementation of argsort / the C extension code invoked by _wrapfunc for handling datetime/NaT values and recent changes to that code; if the crash only occurs with the built extensions in this CI environment, verify the extension build flags and rebuild with sanitizers enabled to pinpoint the invalid memory access.
